LAW AND MOTION 301, RE: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S APPLICATION FOR A RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER AND A WRIT OF ATTACHMENT IS DENIED. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT TO DEPT. 301 A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

calendar.matters:
MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-01-2026 Off Calendar

APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
Off Calendar May-14-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF VISTACORE PARTNERS, LLC's, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's application for a right to attach order and a writ of attachment is DENIED.

First, section 5.7 of the parties' agreement states that New York law governs the parties' agreement. Plaintiff ignores this provision and does not brief New York law or explain why it does not apply. (Iucker Decl., Ex. B.)

Second, the application also fails under California law. "The requirements for obtaining a writ of attachment are: (1) the claim upon which the attachment is based is one upon which an attachment may be issued; (2) the plaintiff has established the probable validity of the claim; (3) the attachment is not sought for a purpose other than the recovery of the claim upon which the attachment is based; and (4) the amount to be secured by the attachment is greater than zero." (California Retail Portfolio Fund GMBH & Co. v. Hopkins Real Estate Grp. (2011) 193 Cal.App.4th 849, 856, fn.3, citing Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 484.090, subds. (a)(1)-(4); see also id., Sec. 483.010.) Attachment is authorized for claims for money based upon a contract for a fixed or readily ascertainable amount not less than $500. (Id., Sec. 483.010, subd. (a).)

Plaintiff fails to demonstrate that it is entitled to $281,677.63; that amount is not "readily ascertainable." Plaintiff's manager declares that the value of the purchased receipts is $281,677.63. (Iucker Decl., par. 13.) There is no explanation how he arrived at this amount nor a reasoned calculation regarding defendant's fluctuating cash receipts. The manager merely refers to the April 2026 Notice, which was prepared by plaintiff's counsel who likely lacks a foundation to determine the amount of cash receipts. (Icuker Decl., Ex. D.)

Lastly, plaintiff's fallback demand for unjust enrichment for $131,677.63 further demonstrates that the amount due is not readily ascertainable. Unjust enrichment is not an action on a contract for which attachment is available as a prejudgment remedy. Plaintiff also fails to show the probable validity of the unjust enrichment claim based on the instant record.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit to Dept. 301 a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)
